notes: Student-t likelihood, quadratic mean (alpha + beta1*x + beta2*x^2)
rationale: iter 0 NLPD=2.2482 with normal linear; data has 4 extreme outliers (responses ±11-15) and curved mean; Student-t handles outliers, quadratic captures nonlinearity

notes: Student-t, cubic mean, log-linear heteroscedastic sigma
rationale: iter 1 NLPD=1.5023 (quadratic+t); dataset has heteroscedastic noise; adding cubic term for nonlinear flexibility, log-linear sigma(x) to model variance changing with predictor

notes: Student-t, heteroscedastic, Fourier basis mean (sin/cos at 1x, 2x frequencies)
rationale: iter 2 NLPD=1.1558 with cubic+hetero+t; pattern looks oscillatory; Fourier basis more appropriate than polynomial for oscillatory nonlinearity and avoids boundary explosion

notes: Student-t, quartic polynomial mean, quadratic log-sigma
rationale: iter 2 NLPD=1.1558 best (cubic+linear-hetero); iter 3 Fourier=1.2247 worse; extending cubic to quartic + quadratic log-sigma for more flexible variance shape

notes: Gaussian contamination mixture (pi_out * Normal(mu, sigma_out) + (1-pi) * Normal(mu, sigma)), cubic mean, linear log-sigma
rationale: iter 2 NLPD=1.1558 best; iters 3,4 worse; 4 extreme outliers (|y|>11) could be better modeled by explicit contamination mixture than Student-t, giving tighter predictive distributions for typical points

notes: Contamination mixture, Student-t clean component (nu~Gamma(4,0.5)), cubic+sin(x) mean, linear log-sigma
rationale: iter 5 NLPD=1.1244 best (mixture+cubic+Normal clean); adding Student-t for clean component to handle mild deviations; adding sin(x) to mean since data pattern looks sinusoidal

notes: Contamination mixture + Normal clean, cubic+sin(x) mean, linear log-sigma
rationale: iter 5 NLPD=1.1244 best (cubic+mixture); iter 6=1.1520 had both t-clean and sin(x); testing sin(x) alone with Normal clean to isolate whether sinusoidal mean component helps

notes: Contamination mixture + quartic polynomial (centered at x=2), linear log-sigma
rationale: iter 5 NLPD=1.1244 best (cubic+mixture); iter 4 tested quartic without mixture=1.2319; now combining quartic with mixture; centering predictor reduces polynomial collinearity
